intro: Find a comfortable seated position. You may sit on a cushion, a chair, or the floor.
Gently straighten your spine. Imagine a thread pulling the crown of your head toward the sky.
Roll your shoulders back and down. Let your hands rest naturally on your knees or in your lap.
Tuck your chin slightly inward. Feel your neck long and aligned with your spine.
Softly close your eyes, or lower your gaze to a point on the floor in front of you.
Now take a slow, deep breath in through your nose. Fill your lungs completely.
Exhale slowly through your mouth. Let the exhale be longer than the inhale.
Again, breathe in deeply. Feel your belly expand as the air fills you.
And breathe out, slowly, gently. Feel your body soften with each exhale.
Begin to slow your breathing down. Let each breath become a little longer, a little deeper.
Notice the cool air entering your nostrils, and the warm air leaving.
With each breath, feel yourself settling more deeply into stillness.
Let your breathing find its own slow, natural rhythm. There is nothing to force.
Bring your full awareness to this breath. Just this one breath, right now.
body_scan: Bring your attention to the top of your head. Notice any sensations there without judgment.
Let your awareness drift down to your forehead and temples. Soften any tension you find.
Notice your eyes, your cheeks, your jaw. Allow your jaw to release and hang slightly open.
Feel your neck and throat. With each exhale, let them soften a little more.
Bring awareness to your shoulders. Let them drop away from your ears.
Notice your arms, your elbows, your wrists, your hands. Let them feel heavy and relaxed.
Feel your chest rising and falling with each breath. There is nothing to effort here.
Bring attention to your belly. Let it be soft and open with each inhale.
Notice your lower back and hips. Allow them to release into the surface beneath you.
Feel your thighs, your knees, your calves. Let them rest completely.
Bring awareness to your ankles, your feet, your toes. Feel the ground supporting you.
Now hold your entire body in awareness. Feel it as one whole, breathing and alive.
anchors: Gently bring your attention back to your breath. Feel the air entering and leaving your nostrils.
If your mind has wandered, that is perfectly okay. Simply return to the sensation of breathing.
Notice the pause between your inhale and exhale. Rest in that stillness for a moment.
Feel the gentle rise and fall of your chest. Let this rhythm be your anchor.
You are here, in this moment. Let the breath remind you of that.
Each time you notice your mind drifting, smile inwardly and return to the breath.
There is nowhere else to be. Just here, just breathing, just this moment.
closing: Begin to notice the stillness within you. This calm feeling is always available to you.
Stay with this sense of peace for a moment. There is no rush to leave.
Feel the quiet space you have created inside. It belongs to you.
Gently begin to deepen your breath. Let each inhale bring a little more energy.
Slowly bring movement back to your fingers and toes. Small, gentle movements.
When you are ready, softly open your eyes. Take in the space around you with fresh awareness.
Carry this feeling of calm with you as you return to your day. Namaste.
